name: Lava Network Decentralized RPC API
what_it_does: Decentralized RPC infrastructure network connecting blockchain node providers with developers — offering censorship-resistant, load-balanced access to 30+ blockchain networks through a token-incentivized protocol, providing an alternative to centralized RPC providers.
use_cases: Accessing Ethereum and Cosmos chain RPC without single-provider dependency or censorship risk
Building censorship-resistant dApps that route requests across multiple node providers
Multi-chain RPC access with automatic failover and load balancing via decentralized network
Querying blockchain state for agents that require decentralization guarantees
Cost-effective RPC access leveraging competitive node provider market
not_for: Applications requiring guaranteed response times from specific geographic nodes
Ultra-low latency MEV strategies requiring co-located infrastructure
Teams unfamiliar with decentralized infrastructure and its tradeoffs
best_when: An agent needs censorship-resistant, decentralized RPC access across multiple chains without relying on a single centralized provider.
avoid_when: You need SLA guarantees, specific node provider selection, or ultra-low latency for time-sensitive operations.

auth.notes: API key from Lava Gateway dashboard. Standard Bearer token or URL parameter. Same familiar JSON-RPC interface as centralized providers. Gateway abstracts decentralized routing.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier available; exact limits depend on network capacity
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-use based on compute units; token staking for discounts
pricing.notes: Token-incentivized network — LAVA token used for protocol. Gateway provides familiar Web2 pricing interface. Early-stage protocol with growing node provider ecosystem. Competitive with centralized providers.

performance.rate_limits: Based on subscription tier and network capacity

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON-RPC standard errors for chain queries. Gateway documentation covers common chains. Decentralized routing means error patterns may vary by provider. Network-level errors less deterministic than centralized providers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Chain read operations idempotent. Write operations (eth_sendRawTransaction) follow blockchain semantics. Gateway routing is transparent to caller.
known_agent_gotchas: Newer network — provider coverage and reliability still maturing vs. Alchemy/Infura
Decentralized routing means response times less predictable than centralized providers
Token economics add complexity for pure API usage — use Gateway to abstract this
Chain support growing — verify your target chain is fully supported and stable

security_score_components.security_notes: Open-source protocol. TLS on Gateway endpoints. API key auth at gateway layer. Decentralized network means no single point of control. Early-stage — security audit coverage growing.
